analyze.title: Analyzing a resonance CSV
analyze.body: Capture data on the printer with TEST_RESONANCES (or use 🔴 Live tools), then load the .csv here. FilaMind runs Klipper’s own shaper math and recommends a shaper + frequency. Do the X file and the Y file in turn — they combine into one config block.

grade.title: What the A–F grade means
grade.body: A measurement-quality score from five equal parts: peak clarity, leftover vibration, smoothing, frequency (40–90 Hz is healthy), and how clean the resonance is. A/B → apply it; C → usable, skim the tips; D/F → fix the mechanics and re-test.

diagnostics.title: Reading the diagnostics
diagnostics.body: Each card maps the measurement to a likely mechanical cause and a fix. Lime = healthy, yellow = worth improving, red = fix before trusting the result. Apply a fix, re-run, and watch the card turn green.

chart.title: Reading the frequency chart
chart.body: Solid curves are the measured vibration energy (X+Y+Z total plus each axis). The ▲ marks the dominant resonance. The faint curves are each shaper’s leftover vibration across frequency — the recommended one (pink) dips deepest at your peak.

shapers.title: Choosing a shaper
shapers.body: Each row trades leftover vibration against smoothing. ZV / MZV are lightest (clean single resonance); EI is more robust; 2-/3-hump handle broad or multiple resonances but smooth more. FilaMind highlights the best balance.

config.title: Applying the config
config.body: This [input_shaper] block carries your X and/or Y result. Copy it into printer.cfg and restart Klipper. The badges show which axes are captured — capture both X and Y for a complete block.

noise.title: Noise check
noise.body: Reads the accelerometer’s idle vibration without moving — a quick mount check before testing. Klipper’s rule of thumb: ~1–100 is normal, ~1000+ means a loose sensor or wiring. Fix a noisy mount first or every test is unreliable.

belts.title: Belt comparison (CoreXY)
belts.body: Excites each belt diagonal and overlays the two responses. Matched peak frequencies = even tension. A gap means the lower-frequency belt is looser — tighten it and re-run until the peaks line up.

axesMap.title: Axes-map detection
axesMap.body: Jogs the toolhead a little in X, then Y, then Z to learn how the accelerometer is mounted, and gives you the axes_map line for printer.cfg. Run it once after fitting or moving the sensor so every later test reads the right axes.

sustain.title: Sustain frequency
sustain.body: Holds the toolhead buzzing at one frequency so you can touch belts, the carriage and the frame by hand to find what rattles. The spectrogram + timeline show whether your target frequency is dominating and when a touch quieted it.

vibrations.title: Vibrations profile
vibrations.body: Sweeps many speeds along each motor angle to map where the machine runs smoothest and which speeds hit a resonance. Favour the smoothest speeds for print / travel moves and avoid the flagged ones. Symmetry % shows how well the two motors match.

guided.title: Guided vs. manual
guided.body: Guided walks the whole flow — Noise → Belts → Shaper X → Shaper Y → Vibrations → Pressure — with a pass / fail gate at each step. Use it for a full tune. Switch to 📈 Analyze or 🔴 Live tools when you just want one specific tool.

history.title: History & trends
history.body: Each calibration is saved (in this browser) with its grade and score. The ▲/▼ arrow compares a run to the previous test of the same axis — up means a cleaner capture or a stiffer axis, down means more noise or a softer axis.

inputShaper.def: A filter Klipper applies to motion that cancels the printer’s main vibration, so you can print faster without ringing / ghosting.

resonance.def: The frequency (Hz) at which an axis naturally vibrates when pushed. Lower = softer / heavier axis; ~40–90 Hz is a healthy printer.

psd.def: Power spectral density — how much vibration energy sits at each frequency. The tall peak is the resonance the shaper targets.

smoothing.def: A side-effect of shaping that slightly rounds sharp corners. Lower is better; ≤0.1 is minimal, >0.2 starts to soften detail.

vibrationPct.def: How much resonance is left after the shaper. Lower is better; ≤2% is excellent, ≤5% is fine.

maxAccel.def: The acceleration the shaper can sustain without too much smoothing — a suggested SET_VELOCITY_LIMIT / printer.cfg value.

note.good: Sharp peak, well above the noise.
note.poor: Weak peak — noisy or very stiff.

note.ok: A little soft or very stiff.

verdict.b: Good — solid result, minor room to improve.
verdict.c: Fair — usable; see the suggestions below.
verdict.d: Poor — mechanical tuning + a re-test advised.
verdict.f: Unreliable — re-run the test (check the sensor mount).

noUsable.title: No usable resonance
noUsable.advice: Re-run TEST_RESONANCES. Check the accelerometer is wired and mounted firmly to the toolhead.

noisy.title: Noisy capture
noisy.advice: Secure the accelerometer mount and re-run. Avoid fans or other motion during the test.

lowFreq.title: Low resonance frequency
lowFreq.advice: Soft or heavy axis — tighten the belts and check frame rigidity. A stiffer axis resonates higher and prints faster.

softAxis.title: Somewhat soft axis
softAxis.advice: Frequency is on the low side. Firming up belts or the frame raises it and lets you print faster.

highSmoothing.title: High smoothing
highSmoothing.advice: Will round corners. Pick a stiffer shaper or lower max_accel.

multiResonance.title: Multiple resonances
multiResonance.advice: Often a loose toolhead, belt, or part. Re-seat the toolhead and re-check belt tension.

vibrationRemains.title: Vibration remains
vibrationRemains.advice: Mechanical tuning (belts, frame, toolhead mass) could push this lower.

healthy.title: Healthy axis
healthy.advice: Clean capture and a stiff axis — good to go. Apply the config and restart Klipper.

axesDiffer.title: X and Y differ a lot
axesDiffer.advice: Asymmetric stiffness. Check belt tension on the softer axis; on CoreXY make sure both belts are matched and tight.

matched.title: Belts matched
matched.advice: The two belt responses line up — tension looks balanced.

differ.title: Belts differ
differ.advice: The belt resonances differ — re-tension the looser belt until both peaks line up.

elevated.title: Reduce sensor noise
elevated.why: Max {max} ≥ {threshold}. Turn off the toolhead fan and re-check the mount, then re-run.

bad.title: Fix the accelerometer before testing
bad.why: Noise {max} is too high — re-seat the sensor and check its wiring.

matched.title: Belts balanced
matched.why: Tension is even — calibrate the shaper next.

mismatch.title: Re-tension belt {looser}
mismatch.why: A {peakA} Hz vs B {peakB} Hz (Δ{diffPct}%). Tighten the lower-frequency belt until both peaks line up, then re-run.

lowFreq.title: Re-run at a lower acceleration
lowFreq.why: Too much low-frequency motion was recorded — lower ACCEL (or raise SIZE) so only constant speeds are measured, then re-run.

favour.title: Favour ~{speed} mm/s
favour.why: Smoothest band {start}–{end} mm/s — set slicer print / travel speeds here when you can.

avoid.title: Avoid {shown} mm/s
avoid.why: These speeds hit a resonance — keep print + travel moves away from them in the slicer.

symmetry.title: Check belt tension / motor match
symmetry.why: Motor symmetry {pct}% is low — re-tension the belts (compare belts) and verify both motor currents match.

pressure.title: Run a pressure-advance tower
pressure.why: Print the PA tower, read the height where corners look sharpest, then `SET_PRESSURE_ADVANCE ADVANCE=<value>` and `SAVE_CONFIG`.

verdict.alreadyCorrect: Your configured axes_map is already correct.
verdict.add: Add this so every Input Shaping graph reads X/Y/Z aligned.

noise.label: Noise
noise.title: Accelerometer noise
noise.why: Confirm the sensor is mounted solidly before any test (motion-free).

belts.label: Belts
belts.title: CoreXY belt balance
belts.why: Match the two belt tensions. CoreXY only — skip on a cartesian / bed-slinger.

shaperX.label: Shaper X
shaperX.title: Input shaper — X
shaperX.why: Find the X resonance and the best shaper.

shaperY.label: Shaper Y
shaperY.title: Input shaper — Y
shaperY.why: Find the Y resonance and the best shaper.

vibrations.label: Vibrations
vibrations.title: Vibrations profile
vibrations.why: Sweep speed × motor angle to find the smoothest speeds and the resonances to avoid.

pressure.label: Pressure
pressure.title: Pressure advance
pressure.why: Tune pressure advance last to sharpen corners and seams.

vibrations.lowFreq: Too much low-freq motion — lower accel & re-run

ui.title: 🧭 Guided tune
ui.copyFailed: Copy failed — select the text and copy it manually.
ui.summaryNote: Your X and Y captures are in the combined {code} block below — copy it, then restart Klipper. Re-open guided tune any time to re-check.

widget.intro: Tune input shaping from a Klipper resonance capture — no command line. New here? Start with {guided}. Or pick {analyze} to work a {csv} yourself, {live} to capture on the printer, or {history} to review past runs.
widget.auditEmpty: No results yet — run a tune, or save one to the archive.
widget.noShaper: No shaper recommended for this data.
widget.legendAxisHint: frequency (Hz) → · solid = measured · faint = shaper leftover
widget.pasteHint: Paste into your {cfg}, then restart Klipper.
widget.errCopyFailed: Copy failed — select the text and copy it manually.

fromPrinter.intro: ⏳ These move the toolhead and can take {duration} (a belt comparison runs two full sweeps) — keep this page open until the result appears.
fromPrinter.liveDesc: Homes the printer if needed, then runs TEST_RESONANCES on the chosen axis (needs an accelerometer + a {section}). Refused while printing.
fromPrinter.beltsBtnTitle: CoreXY: excite each belt diagonal and overlay the responses
fromPrinter.beltsDesc: CoreXY only: runs two sweeps along the belt diagonals and overlays them. Refused while printing.
fromPrinter.axesBtnTitle: Jog +X/+Y/+Z to detect the accelerometer orientation (axes_map)
fromPrinter.axesDesc: Jogs ~30 mm in X, then Y, then Z to detect the accelerometer orientation. Refused while printing.
fromPrinter.beltPeaks: A {a} Hz vs B {b} Hz · Δ{diff}%
fromPrinter.beltChartLabel: Belt A vs B frequency response
fromPrinter.sustainDesc: Buzzes the toolhead in place near the frequency — touch belts, the toolhead and frame to find what rattles. Refused while printing.
fromPrinter.energyLegend: energy vs time — the cyan dip is where a touch helped
fromPrinter.vibDesc: Sweeps speed × motor angle around bed center — a long run (minutes). A smaller {step} is finer but slower. Reports the smoothest speeds, the speeds to avoid, motor symmetry and the motor resonance. Refused while printing.
fromPrinter.errCopyFailed: Copy failed — select the text and copy it manually.
fromPrinter.vibReattached: Reattached: this result finished while the page was away.

vibrationsView.title: Vibrations profile
vibrationsView.svgHeatmapLabel: Vibration energy heatmap by angle and speed

compareView.title: Compare two captures (A ⇄ B)
compareView.intro: Before/after a mechanical fix, or the same axis re-tested. (Compare same-axis files.)
compareView.chartAriaLabel: A vs B power spectral density

csvSource.title: CSV source
csvSource.saveTitle: Copy this capture into the archive
csvSource.archiveEmpty: Nothing saved yet — use 💾 save above, or "save to archive" under a config.
